mechanism_narrative: An unprecedented single-month surge in the unemployment rate, far exceeding historical norms, signals a catastrophic economic shock, leading to sharply lower forward earnings expectations and negative equity returns as investors price in a deep and prolonged recession.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2020 UNRATE jumped to 14.7% from 4.4% in March 2020
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive over the subsequent 60 days in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: The month-over-month percentage change in retail sales acts as a high-frequency proxy for consumer demand momentum. A sharp negative change signals a contraction in aggregate demand, lower corporate revenues, and negative forward equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2020 RSAFS showed a record monthly decline due to lockdowns
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 1-month percentage change in retail sales and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant and negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the Zero Lower Bound, traditional monetary policy signals are exhausted. In this regime, any marginal improvement in industrial production, signaling a bottoming of economic contraction, will lead to a strong positive equity market response due to massive liquidity support.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was cut to 0.05% in March 2020
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the correlation between monthly changes in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not positive and significant.

mechanism_narrative: The 3-month rate of change in industrial production reflects momentum in the economy's productive capacity. Positive momentum indicates economic expansion, driving higher earnings and positive equity returns. This is an always-on, trend-following signal.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO has a rolling 3-year rank correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns of less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous sharp decline in payroll employment and stable or rising inflation indicates a stagflationary shock. This combination compresses corporate profit margins from both falling demand and rising input costs, leading to strongly negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change < -2000 and CPIAUCSL YoY change > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:April 2020 PAYEMS fell by over 20 million while CPI inflation remained positive
failure_condition: In periods meeting the trigger condition, 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a key long-leading indicator of economic activity and consumer confidence. A collapse below a critical threshold signals a deep recession and triggers negative forward returns as the market prices in a collapse in residential investment.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,000, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically lower than in periods when HOUST is above 1,000.

mechanism_narrative: The monthly change in nonfarm payrolls is a primary measure of economic health. A significant negative change reflects widespread job loss, immediately reducing aggregate income and consumption expectations, leading to negative equity returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:The April 2020 payrolls report showed a historic loss of 20.5 million jobs
failure_condition: The correlation between the 1-month change in PAYEMS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not positive and significant over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year inflation rate exceeds the nominal federal funds rate, the resulting negative real interest rate makes equities more attractive relative to bonds and cash. This stimulates risk-taking and drives positive forward returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:In April 2020, YoY CPI was ~0.3% while FEDFUNDS was 0.05%
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly higher than in periods where it is not met.
